name: BTC 4h Session Impulse Hold Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: a session-window directional impulse establishes control, the first structural pullback holds, and continuation expands from that control zone
thesis: Session-window continuation is part of the cycle 83 concept set, but ETH 4h is the wrong ownership lane because the regime is pure chop. BTC 4h is the better fit inside the allowed basket: similar enough to the primary lane to stay relevant, but with more transition character so session-control continuation is not structurally absurd.
invalidation_condition: If BTC 4h session-control holds still undertrade or fail immediately after confirmation, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional session impulse establishes control, the first retrace holds above fast structure, and price continues into the next leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition-capable markets often hand off directional control around session boundaries. If the first pullback holds, continuation can be denser and cleaner than rare breakout persistence logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is the best fit because it provides a more plausible session-control continuation lane than ETH chop while staying inside the allowed basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The impulse may already be spent by the time the hold confirms, turning entries into local exhaustion buys.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If count is acceptable but runner contribution is weak, bring the first partial closer before changing the hold grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest number of impulse-hold entries, several first-target wins, and a smaller number of stronger continuation bursts.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h clears density and produces materially more participation than the sparse DOGE session version did.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated hold confirmations that fail immediately.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because control-change plus one-bar hold is a recurring structure

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit similar-asset lane for session-control continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h shows enough impulse-hold expansions with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h session impulses remain too sparse or fail immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with several continuation bursts.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same grammar survives the primary lane even if the regime fit is weaker.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves a smaller but still tradable impulse-hold structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept needs more transition than pure chop offers.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, lower count continuation than BTC.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether stronger trend contamination improves or degrades the same session-control grammar.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h keeps enough impulse-hold continuation without turning into late chase entries.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h converts the setup into exhausted continuation buying.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger carry than BTC.

long: Next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.10 * ATR_14
short: Next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.10 * ATR_14
